mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth remains positive and above historical median.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 PAYEMS at 135,844k indicates ongoing labor market recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS growth decelerates to below 50,000 persons for two consecutive months, or 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for more than one month, monetary tightening constrains credit availability and reduces equity risk appetite. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants price in slower growth and compressed multiples.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 FEDFUNDS at 5.25% indicates elevated policy rate
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signals strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, reflecting joint validation of demand-side momentum.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both show positive month-over-month growth in same month
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 HOUST at 1,772k and RSAFS at 363,673M indicate demand recovery
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the interaction signal and forward returns drops below 0.15 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining more than 1.5% over three months signals supply-side contraction and potential stagflation risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward and demand inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 INDPRO at 113.13 reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
regime_hint: supply-side contraction or stagflation risk regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > 1.5% are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during supply contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when unemployment crosses above this threshold, as it indicates transition from recovery to slowdown regime.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 UNRATE at 4.4% remains below threshold, indicating labor market resilience
regime_hint: labor market deterioration regime with rising unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation, constraining real economic growth. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real rates compress equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS > 4.5% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 CPIAUCSL at 202.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% indicate restrictive policy stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Joint condition of high inflation and high rates fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at above 2.0% annualized rate signals sustained economic expansion and supports equity earnings growth. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when GDP growth remains above potential, reflecting healthy demand environment.
evidence_refs: fact:Q1 2012 GDPC1 at 11,432.9B indicates ongoing expansion
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with above-potential growth
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized, or 60-day forward returns during above-2.0% GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerating above 2.0% year-over-year combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals supply-demand balance is tightening with capacity utilization rising. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors recognize pricing power and margin expansion potential.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 INDPRO at 113.13 and RSAFS at 363,673M reflect post-crisis supply-demand normalization
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized supply-demand tightening
failure_condition: Joint condition of strong industrial production and rising sales fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
